task_completion.description: Core, distribution, and access switching layers are added to the diagram using Cisco shape libraries.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main Drawio diagram page shows a hierarchical network topology with distinct layers of switches (core, distribution, access) represented by standard Cisco device icons.

task_completion.description: Hierarchical color-coding is applied to the network layers.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Shapes or their backgrounds are colored according to the scheme: gold for WAN/ISP, blue for the core layer, green for the distribution layer, and orange for the access layer.

task_completion.description: Inter-layer links are labeled with interface type and capacity.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The connecting lines between the different layers (e.g., core to distribution) have text labels indicating bandwidth, interface type, or protocol (e.g., '40Gbps', 'OSPF').

task_completion.description: A second page named 'OOB Management' is created in the Drawio file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The page tabs at the bottom of the Drawio interface show a second tab explicitly named 'OOB Management'.

task_completion.description: The out-of-band management network is drawn on the second page.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'OOB Management' page contains shapes representing a console server and a management switch, with connection lines routing to routers and core switches.

task_completion.description: The complete diagram is exported as a PDF to ~/Diagrams/enterprise_network.pdf.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A terminal window, file manager, or PDF viewer shows the file 'enterprise_network.pdf' existing in the '~/Diagrams/' directory.

integrity.description: The agent used the Drawio graphical interface to build the diagram.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Drawio application open with the agent actively dragging shapes, editing text, or changing colors, rather than just writing XML code in a text editor.

integrity.description: The agent opened and read the network requirements text file.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show '~/Desktop/network_requirements.txt' opened in a text editor (like gedit, nano, or cat in terminal) to read the specifications.

integrity.description: The PDF was genuinely exported from the Drawio application.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent using the Drawio 'File -> Export as -> PDF' menu or dialog, rather than just renaming a file extension.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The starting diagram has 1 page and 4 shapes (2 ISP clouds, 2 border routers). The final diagram must have exactly 2 pages and at least 20 shapes (adding 2 core, 4 distribution, 8 access, and 2 OOB devices). The required layers are 'core', 'distribution', and 'access'. The second page must be named 'OOB Management'. Color-coding scheme: gold (WAN), blue (core), green (distribution), orange (access). Inter-layer links must be labeled with bandwidth and protocol (e.g., '40Gbps / OSPF'). The final outputs must be saved to ~/Diagrams/enterprise_network.drawio and exported to ~/Diagrams/enterprise_network.pdf.
